e_visa.nationality: 67 countries including USA, Canada, EU member states, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, United Kingdom and others
e_visa.notes: Single-entry e-visa valid for up to 30 days. Applications submitted via e-pasluga.by (service code 3.04.01). Processed within 7 calendar days. No visa support documents required. Valid through all international checkpoints including all 6 airports and road/rail borders. Fee: EUR 35 for EU citizens; EUR 60 for other nationalities; free for Japanese citizens and children under 12 (EU) / under 14 (other). Additional service fee EUR 6 for all applicants. Program launched March 20, 2025. The complete list of 67 eligible countries is in a downloadable PDF on the e-pasluga.by portal.

visa_policy.notes: Airport visa-free entry (30 days) for 76 countries via Minsk, Brest, Gomel, Grodno, Mogilev, and Vitebsk airports, excluding flights from Russia. European countries visa-free regime for road/rail checkpoints valid to December 31, 2026 — Poland/Lithuania/Latvia citizens max 90 days per entry; all other 35 European countries max 30 days per entry. Total stay must not exceed 90 days per calendar year. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months after expected departure with minimum 2 blank pages. For airport entry, financial means required: equivalent of 2 base rates per day or 50 base rates for full 30-day stay, plus medical insurance covering minimum EUR 10,000. Travelers entering under European scheme cannot transit to Russia.

credential.subtype: valid multiple-entry visa of EU member states or Schengen Area (must have been used at least once — border entry stamp required)

conditional_access.conditions: Must hold a valid (not expired) multiple-entry visa of an EU member state or Schengen Area country with an entry stamp proving prior use. Entry and exit only through designated Belarusian airports (not permitted via Russia). Passport valid at least 90 days beyond intended departure. Financial means equivalent to 2 base rates per day or 50 base rates for full stay. Medical insurance minimum EUR 10,000. Air ticket confirming departure from a Belarusian airport within 30 days.

credential.subtype: permanent and temporary residence permit in Russia

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a permanent or temporary residence permit issued by Russia may enter Belarus visa-free. Alternatively, a valid US, Canada or Schengen visa also qualifies. Duration not explicitly specified on official page. Note: El Salvador has no unconditional bilateral visa-free agreement with Belarus; access is credential-based only.

Holders of a valid US visa may enter Belarus visa-free. A valid Canada or Schengen visa also qualifies, as does a Russian residence permit (permanent or temporary). Duration not explicitly specified on official page.

Holders of a valid Canadian visa may enter Belarus visa-free. A valid US or Schengen visa also qualifies, as does a Russian residence permit (permanent or temporary). Duration not explicitly specified on official page.

Holders of a valid Schengen visa may enter Belarus visa-free. A valid US or Canada visa also qualifies, as does a Russian residence permit (permanent or temporary). Duration not explicitly specified on official page.

For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid US visa may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid Canada, Japan, UK, or Schengen visa; OR permanent residence document from US, Canada, Japan, UK, Schengen countries, or Pacific Alliance countries (Chile, Colombia, Mexico, Peru).

For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid Canadian visa may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid US, Japan, UK, or Schengen visa; OR permanent residence from those countries or Pacific Alliance countries.

For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid Japan visa may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid US, Canada, UK, or Schengen visa; OR permanent residence from those countries or Pacific Alliance countries.

For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid UK visa may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid US, Canada, Japan, or Schengen visa; OR permanent residence from those countries or Pacific Alliance countries.

For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid Schengen visa may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid US, Canada, Japan, or UK visa; OR permanent residence from those countries or Pacific Alliance countries.

credential.subtype: valid document confirming permanent residence in the US, Canada, Japan, UK, or Schengen State

conditional_access.conditions: For tourism, business, cultural or sporting events (non-remunerated activities). Holders of a valid permanent residence document from the US, Canada, Japan, UK, or any Schengen country may enter visa-free for up to 180 days. Also qualifies: valid permanent residence from a Pacific Alliance country (Chile, Colombia, Mexico, Peru); or a valid visa from those countries.

credential.subtype: valid multiple entry short-term Schengen visa type C

conditional_access.conditions: Under the bilateral agreement (general page), holders of a valid multiple-entry short-term Schengen visa (Type C) may enter Belarus visa-free for up to 15 days. North Macedonia citizens can also enter without any credential for 30 days under the 38-country European road/rail scheme (valid to Dec 31 2026).

credential.subtype: any valid Russian visa (e-visas excluded due to technical incompatibility)

conditional_access.conditions: Under the Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Belarus and the Government of the Russian Federation on mutual recognition of visas (entered into force January 11, 2025), foreign citizens and stateless persons holding a valid Russian visa may enter, stay in, and transit through Belarus without a separate Belarusian visa, provided they hold an identity document recognized by both states. Stay counted from entry into the Union State from a third country. Does not apply to persons restricted from entering either state. Land border crossings limited to six designated routes. E-visas are excluded from mutual recognition due to technical incompatibility.

credential.subtype: temporary or permanent residence permit issued by Russia

conditional_access.conditions: Under the Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Belarus and the Government of the Russian Federation on mutual recognition of visas (entered into force January 11, 2025), foreign citizens and stateless persons holding a valid Russian temporary or permanent residence permit may enter, stay in, and transit through Belarus without a separate Belarusian visa, provided they hold an identity document recognized by both states. Holders of temporary residence permits: max 90 days per calendar year in Belarus. Does not apply to persons restricted from entering either state.

Holders of biometric diplomatic passports issued by EU member states may enter, leave, and transit through Belarus without visas under the Agreement between the Republic of Belarus and the European Union on the facilitation of the issuance of visas. This is an exception to the general rule that the 38-country European visa-free scheme excludes diplomatic, service, special, and equivalent passport holders.

cbi.notes: As of June 2026, Belarus does not have an operational citizenship-by-investment program. Draft legislation advancing a CBI framework has been reported in the investment-migration industry press (IMI Daily, Savory & Partners), but no official government source has published a finalized, enacted program with official URL, investment thresholds, or application procedures. The proposal remains pre-legislative/draft stage and is not actionable.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Permit by Investment
programs.notes: Foreigners who make investments in Belarus of at least 15,000 basic units (approx. USD 202,000 based on industry estimates; the official page does not state USD equivalent) may obtain a permanent residence permit directly. Eligible investment methods: creating a commercial organization, acquiring/constructing real estate (excluding personal residential use), obtaining intellectual property rights, purchasing shares in commercial organizations, concession-based investments, or public-private partnerships. Applications submitted to the territorial Office of Citizenship and Migration. The official page does not specify a path-to-citizenship timeline from investment-based residency.

programs.name: Hi-Tech Park (HTP) Simplified Residence for Foreign Workers and Founders
programs.notes: Foreign workers and founders of HTP-resident companies enjoy visa-free entry to Belarus with extended temporary stay of up to 180 days. Temporary residence permit process is streamlined. No special work permits required for HTP residents when hiring foreign nationals. Requires establishing a legal entity in Belarus engaged in software development, IT services, or high-tech R&D.

programs.name: Hi-Tech Park (HTP) — Visa-Free and Simplified Residence for IT/Tech Talent
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who are founders or employees of companies that are residents of the Belarus Hi-Tech Park (HTP). Company must be engaged in software development, IT services, or high-tech R&D and registered as a legal entity in Belarus.
programs.notes: Key immigration benefits: (1) Visa-free entry for foreign workers and founders of HTP companies with temporary stay up to 180 days; (2) Simplified temporary residence permit process; (3) No requirement to obtain special work permits when employing foreigners. HTP residency confers additional tax and customs benefits. Official HTP site: park.by

agreements: Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) — freedom of movement / visa-free travel among member states
Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU) — free movement of goods, services, capital, and labor among Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Russia
Union State of Belarus and Russia — full freedom of movement, residence, and work rights between Belarus and Russia without formal immigration procedures

Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Belarus and the Government of the Russian Federation on mutual recognition of visas (signed June 19, 2020; entered into force January 11, 2025) — foreign citizens holding a valid Belarus or Russia visa or residence permit may enter either country without a separate visa for the other; e-visas excluded due to technical incompatibility

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Panama max_stay_days changed from 30 to 180 — official MFA /general/ page confirms 'up to 180 days' under bilateral agreement.
CORRECTED: Peru max_stay_days changed from 30 to 183 — official MFA /general/ page confirms 'up to 183 days for tourism purposes' under bilateral agreement.
CORRECTED: Panama notes updated — prior record cited only airport 30-day entry; the bilateral agreement (general page) grants 180 days unconditionally.
CORRECTED: Peru notes updated — prior record cited only airport 30-day entry; the bilateral agreement (general page) grants 183 days for tourism.

CORRECTED: North Macedonia notes clarified — the European road/rail scheme (38 countries, /europe/ page) grants unconditional 30-day visa-free access; the /general/ page conditions (private invitation / travel company / healthcare institution / Schengen visa) apply to the bilateral channel and are already captured in conditional_access.
CORRECTED: Haiti notes clarified — bilateral agreement (90 days, confirmed) is the primary visa_free entry; airport conditional entry (Schengen visa required) accurately noted.
CORRECTED: e-visa notes enriched with fee structure (EUR 35 EU / EUR 60 others / free Japan) and launch date (March 20, 2025) confirmed from official MFA page.
CORRECTED: Russian residence permit conditional_access max_stay_days updated from null to 90 (per calendar year) for temporary residence permit holders, per the official agreement page.
CORRECTED: Russian visa conditional_access notes updated to explicitly state e-visas are excluded due to technical incompatibility (confirmed from official agreement page).
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: El Salvador — correctly recorded as airport visa-free (30 days) plus credential-based conditional access only from general page; no unconditional bilateral.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: European scheme 38 countries — confirmed valid to Dec 31 2026.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Airport visa-free = 76 countries per main MFA airport page (authoritative over UAE embassy page which cited 74).
gaps: The complete list of 67 e-visa eligible countries is not enumerated on any MFA web page; the official list is only available as a downloadable PDF on e-pasluga.by which was not accessible in full text form.
The airport visa-free country count discrepancy (MFA main page: 76 vs UAE embassy page: 74) could not be fully resolved — the main MFA page is treated as authoritative.

Max stay days for CIS member states (Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, Tajikistan, Ukraine, Uzbekistan) are not explicitly stated on the official MFA page; visa-free status confirmed but duration omitted.

USD/EUR equivalent of the 15,000 basic units investment threshold for permanent residence is not stated on any official page consulted; third-party sources cite approximately USD 202,000 but this is not verifiable from official sources.
Path from investment-based permanent residence to citizenship is not documented on any official page found.
No official Belarus government page was found confirming or detailing a digital nomad visa or formal skilled-worker fast-track program beyond the HTP framework.
The proposed CBI legislation has no official government URL with enacted program details as of the research date.
Diplomatic/service/official passport bilateral visa-waiver agreements are tracked via MFA press releases only; no single comprehensive official list was found.
No official Belarus government source found confirming or denying OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder conditional entry privileges.

The maximum stay duration under the Belarus-Russia Mutual Recognition of Visas agreement for standard visa holders (as opposed to residence permit holders capped at 90 days/year) is not explicitly stated on the official MFA agreement page.

The Belarus-EU Visa Facilitation Agreement enabling biometric diplomatic passport holders of EU member states to enter without visas does not state a specific maximum stay duration on the mfa.gov.by page consulted.
Brest-Grodno 15-day tourist zone — eligible nationalities not enumerated on official gpk.gov.by page; conditions (must book via Belarusian travel agency) confirmed but country scope unclear.

outbound_official.notes: The Belarus MFA page mfa.gov.by/en/visa/freemove/general/ publishes a bilateral outbound visa-free list under the section 'Belarusian citizens with ordinary passports to'. The page carries a disclaimer that information is for reference only and should be confirmed through diplomatic missions. No 'as of' date is shown on the page. Mexico appears as visa-free but entry for up to 180 days is conditional on holding a valid Canada/US/Japan/UK/Schengen visa or Pacific Alliance permanent residence — it is included here as the page lists it under the visa-free section. El Salvador is listed on the same page but only with conditions (Russian/US/Canada/Schengen visa or Russian residence permit) — it is omitted from this outbound_official visa_free list as it has no unconditional bilateral regime for Belarus citizens.
